content.title: Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::8001
content.body:
View details in Rollbar: [https://app.rollbar.com/a/thomas.iniguez/fix/item/FirstProject/33](https://app.rollbar.com/a/thomas.iniguez/fix/item/FirstProject/33)

```

Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::8001
  File "node:net", line 1908, in Server.setupListenHandle [as _listen2]
  File "node:net", line 1965, in listenInCluster
  File "node:net", line 2067, in Server.listen
  File "C:\Users\alpha\AppData\Local\Temp\3AM6P7NubvwTm7PrX4xXyHZkbdy\resources\app.asar\node_modules\express\lib\application.js", line 635, in Function.listen
  File "C:\Users\alpha\AppData\Local\Temp\3AM6P7NubvwTm7PrX4xXyHZkbdy\resources\app.asar\src\index.js", line 588, in <unknown>
```
content.author: alphaleadership

acceptanceCriteria: The application should start successfully without an 'EADDRINUSE' error when no other process is actively using port 8001.

If another instance of the application is detected to be already running on port 8001, the new instance should gracefully handle the conflict (e.g., exit cleanly, prompt the user, or log a clear message).
The application should reliably release port 8001 upon normal shutdown or process termination to prevent lingering port usage.

fileActions.explanation: To investigate how the Express server is initialized, configured, and how the 'listen' call is made on port 8001, as indicated by the stack trace. This will help understand potential causes for the EADDRINUSE error.
